docs.title: Model Backend
docs.text:
SONIC-O1 uses Qwen3-Omni served through vLLM for efficient multi-GPU inference.

Feature Detail Model Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct Serving vLLM with tensor parallelism Context Up to 65 536 tokens Modalities Native video + audio (no transcription needed) Decoding Greedy (temperature=0.0) for deterministic output Caching Prefix caching enabled for follow-up efficiency

The model wrapper (qwen_model.py) handles:

  • Lazy loading and engine recovery after crashes or OOM
  • Chat template formatting via Qwen3OmniMoeProcessor
  • Text-only generation for intermediate reasoning steps
  • Multimodal generation with time-range-aware video and audio slicing

docs.title: Dual Backend: Embedded vs Server Mode
docs.text:
The model wrapper (qwen_model.py) supports two backends, selected by a single config key:

Backend When How Embedded vllm_base_url absent or empty Loads model in-process via vllm.LLM. Original behaviour. Server vllm_base_url set (e.g. http://localhost:8080/v1) Connects to a running vllm serve via OpenAI SDK.

In server mode, video frames are extracted client-side using the same process_video_with_metadata logic as embedded mode (respecting max_frames, min_frames, and time-range slicing), then sent as base64 JPEGs. This gives identical frame sampling behaviour regardless of backend while enabling vLLM's continuous batching for concurrent requests.

The temporal index builder automatically parallelises segment captioning in server mode via ThreadPoolExecutor, sending multiple caption requests simultaneously to the vLLM server.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • CUDA-capable GPUs (4x recommended for tensor parallelism)
  • uv package manager (optional; you can use pip in a container)

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/VectorInstitute/sonic-o1-agent.git
cd sonic-o1-agent

# Install dependencies (with uv)
uv sync --all-extras --group docs --group test

# Or with pip (e.g. inside a Singularity container)
pip install -e ".[docs]"   # installs package + docs deps

# Verify setup
python scripts/verify_setup.py

docs.title: Building the documentation
docs.text:
From the project root, ensure the package is importable when MkDocs runs (required for API reference generation):

# With uv
uv run mkdocs build

# With system/conda Python: add src to PYTHONPATH
PYTHONPATH=src python -m mkdocs build
# Or: PYTHONPATH=src python -m mkdocs serve

docs.title: Basic Inference (Direct Mode)
docs.text:
The simplest mode uses the Planner and Temporal Index to produce a grounded response in a single pass.

python scripts/run_agent.py \
  --video courtroom.mp4 --audio courtroom.m4a \
  --query "Summarize the key arguments"

docs.title: Chain-of-Thought Reasoning
docs.text:
Enables the Reasoner agent for step-by-step analysis with explicit reasoning traces.

python scripts/run_agent.py \
  --video hearing.mp4 \
  --query "Analyze the legal strategy used" \
  --reasoning

docs.title: Self-Reflection
docs.text:
Enables the Reflection agent to evaluate response quality and iteratively refine until the confidence threshold is met.

python scripts/run_agent.py \
  --video deposition.mp4 \
  --query "What inconsistencies exist in the testimony?" \
  --reflection

docs.title: Multi-Step Decomposition
docs.text:
Enables the advanced Planner to automatically decompose complex queries into sequential sub-tasks.

python scripts/run_agent.py \
  --video trial.mp4 \
  --query "Compare the defense attorney's arguments vs the prosecutor's" \
  --multi-step

docs.title: Full Multi-Agent System
docs.text:
Enables all agents working together for maximum capability.

python scripts/run_agent.py \
  --video complex_case.mp4 \
  --query "Provide a comprehensive analysis with key contradictions" \
  --all-features \
  --verbose \
  --output results.json

docs.title: Temporal Queries (Auto-Segmentation)
docs.text:
The Planner agent automatically detects temporal references and segments the video to process only the relevant window.

python scripts/run_agent.py \
  --video hearing.mp4 \
  --query "What happened between minute 5 and 10?"

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: All settings are in configs/agent_config.yaml.

docs.title: Model Settings
docs.text:
model:
  model_path: "Qwen/Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct"
  use_thinking: false

  # vLLM backend mode (optional):
  #   Set to connect to a running vllm serve instance.
  #   Empty or omitted = embedded mode (loads model in-process).
  # vllm_base_url: "http://localhost:8080/v1"

  gpu_memory_utilization: 0.85
  tensor_parallel_size: 4        # Number of GPUs
  max_num_seqs: 1
  max_model_len: 65536

  generation_config:
    temperature: 0.0             # Greedy decoding
    top_p: 0.95
    top_k: 20
    max_new_tokens: 8192

  max_frames: 128
  min_frames: 64

docs.title: Processing Settings
docs.text:
processing:
  max_frames: 128
  max_audio_chunks: null         # null = no chunking
  audio_chunk_duration_sec: 10.0
  min_video_duration_for_segmentation: 300  # 5 minutes
  segment_efficiency_threshold: 0.8

docs.title: Temporal Index Settings
docs.text:
Controls the frame-captioning index that provides temporal grounding. See Architecture -- Temporal Grounding for details on how this works.

temporal_index:
  min_duration_sec: 180          # Skip indexing for short videos
  num_segments: 10               # Segments to split video into
  max_frames_per_segment: 16     # Frames per segment caption
  caption_max_tokens: 256        # Max tokens per caption

Short videos

Videos shorter than min_duration_sec skip the temporal indexing step entirely and rely on sampling-context hints instead. The model can process the entire clip in a single pass without needing segment-level grounding.

docs.title: Reasoning Settings
docs.text:
reasoning:
  max_reasoning_steps: 5
  enable_verification: true

docs.title: Reflection Settings
docs.text:
reflection:
  confidence_threshold: 0.7      # Minimum confidence to accept
  max_refinement_attempts: 2
  use_iterative_refinement: false  # true = loop until ACCEPT or threshold
  check_hallucination: false       # true = run hallucination check after response

docs.title: Planning Settings
docs.text:
planning:
  max_steps: 10   # Max sub-tasks when multi-step decomposes (e.g. compare/contrast)

docs.title: SLURM Submission
docs.text:
For cluster environments, use the provided SLURM script:

# Edit the script with your paths and resource requirements
vim slurm/run_sonic_agent_native.sh

# Submit
sbatch slurm/run_sonic_agent_native.sh

# Monitor output
tail -f logs/sonic_agent_*.out

docs.title: Demo UI
docs.text:
▶ Watch the demo video

The demo provides a web interface for uploading videos and querying the multi-agent pipeline. It streams real-time progress as each agent completes — planning, temporal indexing, inference, and reflection — with a live thinking panel showing intermediate results.

docs.title: Running the Demo
docs.text:
The demo connects to a vLLM server via the OpenAI-compatible API. This separates model serving from the application, enabling continuous batching and parallel temporal indexing.

Step 1 — Start the vLLM server:

vllm serve Qwen/Qwen3-Omni-30B-A3B-Instruct \
  --port 8080 --host 0.0.0.0 \
  --dtype bfloat16 --max-model-len 32768 \
  --allowed-local-media-path / \
  -tp 1

Adjust -tp to match your GPU count (e.g., -tp 4 for 4 GPUs with --max-model-len 65536). Wait for Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:8080.

Step 2 — Start the demo server (separate terminal, same node):

PYTHONPATH=src python -c "
from sonic_o1_agent.api import serve
serve(
    vllm_base_url='http://localhost:8080/v1',
    port=8000,
    config_path='configs/agent_config.yaml',
)
"

Step 3 — Open in your browser:

http://localhost:8000

If running on a remote server or HPC cluster, forward the port:

ssh -L 8000:<node>:8000 user@login-server

docs.title: Server Mode from CLI
docs.text:
You can also use the vLLM server backend from the CLI (without the demo UI). Add vllm_base_url to your config:

model:
  vllm_base_url: "http://localhost:8080/v1"

Or pass it as an environment variable:

VLLM_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8080/v1 python scripts/run_agent.py \
  --config configs/agent_config.yaml \
  --video video.mp4 --audio audio.m4a \
  --query "Summarize the key points" \
  --all-features

Backward compatibility

When vllm_base_url is empty or absent, the system uses the embedded vLLM engine (loads model in-process). All existing CLI usage is completely unchanged.

docs.title: CLI Reference
docs.text:
usage: run_agent.py [-h] [--config CONFIG] [--video VIDEO] [--audio AUDIO]
                    --query QUERY [--max-frames MAX_FRAMES]
                    [--max-audio-chunks MAX_AUDIO_CHUNKS]
                    [--reasoning] [--reflection] [--multi-step]
                    [--all-features] [--output OUTPUT] [--verbose] [--stream]

Arguments:
  --config        Path to config YAML (default: configs/agent_config.yaml)
  --video         Path to video file
  --audio         Path to audio file
  --query         Query/question to ask (required)
  --max-frames    Override max frames to process
  --max-audio-chunks  Override max audio chunks

Agent Modes:
  --reasoning     Enable Chain-of-Thought reasoning
  --reflection    Enable self-reflection and refinement
  --multi-step    Enable multi-step task decomposition
  --all-features  Enable all advanced features

Output:
  --output        Save results to JSON file
  --verbose       Show detailed reasoning trace
  --stream        Stream workflow progress (print each node as it runs; useful in job logs)
